title: GeophysicalEvent
description: An instantaneous or short-interval measurement from a geophysical monitoring source — NOAA SWPC space weather, USGS earthquakes, or JPL HORIZONS ephemerides. Adopted in Session 5 (Wave 2 ingest).

known_failure_modes: Interpreting the 1-minute estimated Kp as the 3-hour planetary Kp — they are separate products and are not comparable at the tail.
Fetching HORIZONS without target/observer/frame specified — the API returns different values for the same 'planet position' depending on reference frame.
Reporting USGS earthquake magnitude without specifying magnitude type (mww, ml, md) — magnitudes on different scales are not directly comparable.
Assuming an SWPC JSON file update means fresh data — some products update timestamps without new observations.

schema_version.description: Semver. Major bump on breaking change; minor on additive; patch on cosmetic.

event_kind.description: The kind of geophysical measurement this record reports. kp_index_3h and kp_index_1m are separate NOAA products and must never be compared directly.

timestamp_utc.description: ISO-8601 UTC. Must include the 'Z' suffix or explicit +00:00 offset. Local times are rejected.

value.description: The measurement value, in the unit named by 'unit'.

magnitude_type.description: Required in practice for earthquake_magnitude records. USGS magnitude scale used, e.g. 'mww', 'ml', 'md', 'mb'. Magnitudes on different scales are not directly comparable.

target_body.description: For HORIZONS: the body whose position/state is reported, e.g. 'Sun', 'Moon', 'Mars'.

reference_frame.description: For HORIZONS: the observer/center and frame convention used, e.g. '@sun', '500@399', '500@10'. Required to interpret target_body positions correctly.

position.description: Optional WorldCoordinateEvent-style position object, inlined here to avoid a cross-schema $ref. For solar-wind measurements, refers to the spacecraft location; for earthquakes, the hypocenter; for planet positions, geocentric or heliocentric coordinates.

lat_deg.description: Latitude in degrees. Positive north. For heliocentric/geocentric planet positions, may represent ecliptic latitude.

lon_deg.description: Longitude in degrees. Positive east. For heliocentric/geocentric planet positions, may represent ecliptic longitude.

alt_m.description: Altitude/depth in meters where applicable (e.g. earthquake hypocenter depth converted to meters).

frame.description: Reference frame for the position. Earthquake hypocenters use WGS84_ellipsoid; HORIZONS vectors use heliocentric_ecliptic or geocentric_equatorial; solar-wind spacecraft use spacecraft_ephemeris.

uncertainty_m.description: 1-sigma position uncertainty in meters, when known (e.g. USGS horizontal error).

source.description: Provenance chain. Required on every record.

primary.description: The authoritative record, e.g. 'NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center', 'USGS Earthquake Hazards Program', 'JPL Horizons System'.

aggregator.description: Any intermediary. Nullable — these three sources are fetched directly, so this is typically null.

fetched_at_utc.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the fetch that produced this record.

license.description: 'public-domain-USG' for NOAA/USGS (17 U.S.C. § 105), 'public-domain-NASA' for JPL HORIZONS.

citation.description: Verbatim courtesy citation string for this record's source, per UPSTREAM_TERMS.md.
